description_long: This is a weathered stone obelisk, likely serving as a grave marker or memorial, with a pointed arched top and a rectangular base. The front face features faint, eroded inscriptions and a decorative relief motif near the top, possibly floral or foliate in design. The stone surface is mottled with patches of lichen, moss, and discoloration, indicating significant age and exposure to the elements. The sides are narrow and plain, showing the thickness of the monument, while the back is largely undecorated and heavily stained. The base is a simple, solid rectangular plinth, slightly wider than the shaft, providing stability. Overall, the object exudes a somber, historical character typical of old cemetery monuments.
description: A weathered stone obelisk with a pointed arched top, faded inscriptions, and a decorative relief on the front, set on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched top, faded inscriptions, and a decorative relief motif above the inscription panel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow thickness of the obelisk and the rectangular base, with visible weathering.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, heavily stained surface with no visible inscriptions or ornamentation.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the narrow profile and weathered stone texture.
attribute_inscription: Faint, eroded inscriptions are present on the front face, in a serif style, likely in English. The text is largely illegible due to weathering.
attribute_ornamentation: A decorative relief motif, possibly floral or foliate, is carved into the upper front panel above the inscription.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, solid rectangular plinth, slightly wider than the shaft, with no visible decorative features. It appears to be made of the same stone as the obelisk.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is pointed with a Gothic-style arched top, smoothly transitioning from the shaft, with no additional decoration.
